title: Zankoku na tenshi no you ni shounen yo shinwa ni nare
display_title: Zankoku na tenshi no you ni shounen yo shinwa ni nare
wikitext:
[]: Zankoku na tenshi no you ni, shounen yo shinwa ni nare!             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Oct 28, 2004 10:43 pm Does the cruel angel refer to Lucifer? If so, is this suggesting that it is Shinji become a 'legend' like him? Really, I think I'm just being an idiot here, but what other cruel angel could they be reffering to?

[]:             Quote Post by aranami » Thu Oct 28, 2004 11:51 pm How about the Watchers http://reluctant-messenger.com/1enoch01 ... #Chapter07

[]: Re: Zankoku na tenshi no you ni, shounen yo shinwa ni nare!             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 29, 2004 1:08 am Dave wrote: Does the cruel angel refer to Lucifer?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Oct 29, 2004 8:18 am Reichu wrote: (and often, incorrectly, as "Lucifer", due to a misreading of Isaiah 14:12).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 29, 2004 9:07 am Dave wrote: Reichu wrote: (and often, incorrectly, as "Lucifer", due to a misreading of Isaiah 14:12).

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Oct 29, 2004 11:14 am So the true name of the angel Satan once was is Sammael (or Samael)?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 29, 2004 12:24 pm Dave wrote: So the true name of the angel Satan once was is Sammael (or Samael)?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Fri Oct 29, 2004 3:58 pm In the bible dosent it say that Lucifer is the angel of music/praise?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Oct 29, 2004 4:33 pm Dark FireStar wrote: In the bible dosent it say that Lucifer is the angel of music/praise?

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Fri Oct 29, 2004 10:32 pm It's possible that the song could refer to Lucifer in some ways. I think we might be reading too much into the lyrics of the song. While it's obvious it relates to Shinji ( :shock: Or does it?), I think that while writing the song, Lucifer probably wasn't any kind of influence. That's just me tho...I've never liked picking apart songs for hidden meanings. I listen to stuff like Pink Flyod and the Beatles not for finding new or hidden meanings, but to listen to some awsome musicians practicing their craft. Now, if you can find some kind of strange mathmatical theory within the notes and barlines of "Cruel Angel's Thesis", then I might be more inclined to see some new meaning. (That is, of course, If I can understand it. T_T I'm so bad at math)

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Oct 29, 2004 10:39 pm Um... I don't think trying to figure out what (or who) the cruel angel is constitutes "picking apart the song for hidden meanings". It seems like a pretty blatant detail to me. They must have had SOMEONE (or something) in mind when this song was written!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 29, 2004 11:43 pm Um... Did any of you folks (besides Dave, maybe) read the links I posted? In case you did not, here are two more: http://www.lds-mormon.com/lucifer.shtml http://www.theosophy-nw.org/theosnw/wor ... -ibel2.htm "Lucifer" is *not* another name for Satan, but this misconception is so widespread that pretty much nothing can be done about it. Alternatively, Satan and Sammael are, from what I understand, the same dude. But the lore surrounding this guy is ridiculously convoluted, far worse than the case of Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Mon Nov 01, 2004 4:05 pm Reichu wrote: Um... Did any of you folks (besides Dave, maybe) read the links I posted? In case you did not, here are two more: http://www.lds-mormon.com/lucifer.shtml http://www.theosophy-nw.org/theosnw/wor ... -ibel2.htm "Lucifer" is *not* another name for Satan, but this misconception is so widespread that pretty much nothing can be done about it. Alternatively, Satan and Sammael are, from what I understand, the same dude. But the lore surrounding this guy is ridiculously convoluted, far worse than the case of Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Nov 01, 2004 7:36 pm i was under the impression that lucifer was an angel that rebelled against god, and in the inferno, was frozen at the very bottom of hell...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 02, 2004 4:33 am sadsadshinji wrote: i was under the impression that lucifer was an angel that rebelled against god, and in the inferno, was frozen at the very bottom of hell...

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Nov 02, 2004 9:43 am sorry about that... the babylonian king? could tie to eva? however, it was a very interesting read...

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Nov 02, 2004 12:10 pm Reichu wrote: There IS no angel named "Lucifer".

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Tue Nov 02, 2004 2:43 pm I wonder if there has been anyone who has ever named there kid "Lucifer"? I wonder if the other angels banned from heven have names also. (I know they probilty do but what?)

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Nov 02, 2004 6:30 pm well...satan was a banished angel and thats why he decided to try to keep others from reaching heaven.... and i think that for all practical purposes, satan = lucifer because of the culture that has grown up around it...

[]:             Quote Post by aranami » Wed Nov 03, 2004 8:47 am THE WATCHERS Samyaza - Leader of the Watchers Azazyel - taught men to make swords, knives, shields, breastplates, the fabrication of mirrors, and the workmanship of bracelets and ornaments, the use of paint, the beautifying of the eyebrows, the use of stones of every valuable and select kind, and all sorts of dyes Amazarak - taught all the sorcerers, and dividers of roots Armers - taught the solution of sorcery Barkaya - taught Astrology Akibeel - taught signs Tamiel - taught astronomy Asaradel - taught the motion of the moon Their actions lead to Noah's flood.
